demographics.occupation: employed — works in a role where they try to keep pace with everyone else and avoid being seen as needing special treatment
demographics.living_situation: lives with a roommate, who is present

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: I pushed myself to work exactly like everyone else today to prove I'm not broken, and now my muscles are twitching uncontrollably and I can't stand up.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: After a couple of days of really pushing through work, the twitching and weakness came on and now I can't get off the bed. I'm scared something's actually wrong and I couldn't wait for a normal appointment.

symptom_narrative.onset: built up over the last day or two of pushing far past what I'd normally do; the twitching and collapse hit hardest today
symptom_narrative.duration: about 48 hours of overdoing it, severe since this morning
symptom_narrative.character: the muscles all over my legs are twitching and cramping, they feel really sore and heavy, and there's no strength there — my legs just give way when I try to stand
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the worst crash I've ever had — I'm completely wiped out and can't get up
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: mostly my thighs and calves, but I feel wrecked all over
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: constant since it came on, it's not easing off
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: lying flat and staying still helps a little
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: any attempt to move or stand — I tried to sit up and immediately failed
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: I feel shaky and drained, my heart feels like it's going a bit fast, and I keep crying and apologising
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: I've had crashes before when I overdo things, but never anything like this
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: I'm stuck in bed — I can't get to the toilet or get myself a drink without help right now

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: a long-term health condition / disability I've had for years that limits what my body can physically handle
past_medical_history.how_managed: usual daily medications, and I normally have a cane and joint braces, though I'd stopped using them around other people

current_medications_as_reported.name_as_patient_states: my regular daily medications for my long-term condition

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: I work hard to keep up with everyone and I don't want to be treated as if I need special allowances. I skipped my afternoon dose today trying to push through the shift. I live with a roommate who's here helping me. I've been feeling worthless about how little I'm getting done, like my value depends on what I produce, and I keep apologising for being a burden.

objective_data_offered.value: you can see the muscles in my thighs and calves twitching and rippling on their own
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: patient/roommate observation over video, not a clinical examination

objective_data_offered.value: my heart feels like it's beating faster than normal and I feel shaky
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: subjective sensation, no home heart-rate device reported

objective_data_offered.value: my roommate says I can't stand or transfer to a chair or the toilet on my own and I look exhausted and tearful
my urine looks normal to me — I haven't noticed it going dark
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: self-observation only; has not passed urine recently and cannot easily reach the toilet
